channel.title: Development on HAL Management Console
channel.description: Recent content in Development on HAL Management Console

item.title: Build & Run
item.description: This page explains how to build, run & debug the console. Build If not already done, clone the code from https://github.com/hal/hal.next/ or fork the repository into your own personal GitHub account. HAL uses Maven for its build. Run Debug Build & run - Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consetetur sadipscing elitr, sed diam nonumy eirmod tempor invidunt ut labore et dolore magna aliquyam erat, sed diam voluptua. At vero eos et accusam et justo duo dolores et ea rebum.

item.title: Architecture
item.description: HAL is a client side RIA without server side dependencies. It is a GWT application - which means it’s written almost completely in Java. GWT is used to transpile the Java code into a bunch of JavaScript, HTML and CSS files. HAL uses some external JavaScript libraries as well. These dependencies are managed using bower which is in turn integrated into the Maven build using the maven-frontend-plugin. Take a look at the bower.

item.title: Building Blocks
item.description: Pending MVP Config Core DMR Metadata MBUI User Interface
